title: anthemis stiparum subsp. sabulicola
intro: Anthemis stiparum subsp. sabulicola, also known as a coastal chamomile, is a subspecies of daisy-like flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and essential oils. The health benefits of this plant include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. Anthemis stiparum subsp. sabulicola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or oil, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
